artifact_title: CloudOps Agentic AI powered by Amazon Bedrock AgentCore

findings.description: Package 'minimatch' version 3.1.2 has a known vulnerability (CVE-2026-27903). This Regular Expression Denial of Service (ReDoS) vulnerability occurs due to unbounded recursive backtracking in the matchOne() function when processing glob patterns with multiple non-adjacent GLOBSTAR segments. Applications can stall for several seconds when processing malicious patterns.
findings.recommendation: Update the minimatch package to version 3.1.3 or later. Run 'npm update minimatch' or modify package.json dependencies to require minimatch version 3.1.3 or higher, then regenerate package-lock.json with 'npm install'.

findings.description: MCP Runtime configured with NetworkMode: 'PUBLIC' exposes the service directly to the internet. Found: 'NetworkConfiguration: { NetworkMode: 'PUBLIC' }' on Billing MCP Runtime (line 162), Pricing MCP Runtime (line 214), CloudWatch MCP Runtime (line 293), CloudTrail MCP Runtime (line 374), and Inventory MCP Runtime (line 500). While JWT authorization is configured, public network exposure increases the attack surface and violates defense-in-depth principles.
findings.recommendation: Change NetworkMode to PRIVATE and place the runtimes in a VPC with private subnets. Access should be routed through an Application Load Balancer or API Gateway in public subnets, which then forwards authenticated requests to the private runtimes. This provides network-level isolation in addition to application-level JWT authentication. Update the NetworkConfiguration to specify VPC configuration with private subnet IDs and security groups that restrict inbound traffic to only the load balancer or API Gateway.

findings.description: Wildcard Resource ('*') combined with write/delete actions grants overly broad permissions. The policy allows creating, deleting, and managing OAuth credential providers and token vaults on all resources. Customers copying this CDK code will grant their Lambda functions admin-equivalent access to AgentCore credential management across their entire account.
findings.recommendation: Scope the resources to specific ARN patterns where the credential providers and token vaults will be created. If the resource ARNs are known at deployment time (e.g., derived from stack name or other identifiers), use those specific ARNs. If the ARNs are only known at runtime, consider whether all these actions truly require wildcard resources or if a subset can be scoped. Add an inline comment explaining why any remaining wildcards are necessary and cannot be scoped.

findings.description: Wildcard Action ('*') combined with wildcard Resource ('*') in DENY policy grants admin-equivalent denial scope. The unauthenticated role policy uses Effect: DENY with actions: ['*'] and resources: ['*'], which creates an overly broad denial that could interfere with legitimate service operations or future role modifications.
findings.recommendation: Remove the explicit DENY policy from the unauthenticated role entirely. The role should have no permissions granted (no Allow statements), which achieves the same security outcome without the risk of overly broad denials. An empty policy or a policy with no statements is sufficient to prevent unauthenticated access. If an explicit deny is required for compliance documentation, scope it to specific high-risk actions rather than all actions.

findings.description: Wildcard Resource ('*') combined with extensive write/delete actions grants overly broad permissions. The policy allows creating and deleting policy engines, policies, and managing gateways on all resources. Actions like CreatePolicyEngine, DeletePolicyEngine, CreatePolicy, and DeletePolicy are destructive operations that should be scoped to specific resource ARNs. Customers copying this pattern will grant their Lambda functions the ability to manipulate any AgentCore policy engine or gateway in their account.
findings.recommendation: Scope the resources to specific ARN patterns. The policy engine ARN is available as a custom resource output (policyEngineArn), and the gateway ARN is constructed at line 558 (gatewayResourceWildcard). Use these specific ARNs instead of wildcards. For actions that genuinely require account-level scope (like ListPolicyEngines), separate them into a distinct policy statement with wildcard resources and add a comment explaining the API limitation. Restrict the write/delete actions to the specific policy engine and gateway resources created by this stack.

findings.description: Wildcard resource ('*') combined with broad write/mutating actions including ce:*, budgets:*, compute-optimizer:*, cost-optimization-hub:*, and multiple EC2/Lambda/ECS describe actions. While Cost Explorer and Budgets APIs require wildcard resources, the policy grants excessive permissions beyond what's needed for a billing MCP server (e.g., ec2:DescribeInstances, lambda:ListFunctions, ecs:ListServices are not billing-specific).
findings.recommendation: Split this policy into two statements: (1) Cost Explorer and Budgets actions with Resource: '*' and a comment explaining these services require wildcard resources, and (2) EC2/Lambda/ECS describe actions scoped to specific resource ARNs or removed if not needed for the billing functionality. Review whether compute-optimizer:* and cost-optimization-hub:* wildcards are necessary or can be restricted to specific actions.

findings.description: Package 'semver' version 6.3.1 has a known high-severity vulnerability (CVE-2022-25883, CVSS 7.5). This Regular Expression Denial of Service (ReDoS) vulnerability in the 'new Range' function can cause denial of service when processing untrusted user data. The vulnerability affects all versions before 7.5.2 on the 7.x branch and before 6.3.1 on the 6.x branch.
findings.recommendation: Update the semver package to version 7.5.2 or later. Run 'npm update semver' or modify package.json to require semver version 7.5.2 or higher, then regenerate package-lock.json with 'npm install'.
